title: The Spice Drawer
text_zh: 我的厨房里有一个专门放调料的抽屉。每次打开它都能闻到各种味道混在一起的香气——八角的甜、花椒的麻、干辣椒的辣，还有桂皮那种温暖的木头味。这些调料大部分是妈妈从老家寄来的，她觉得超市卖的不如家乡的好。每隔几个月她就会寄一个包裹，里面装着用塑料袋分装好的各种调料，每个袋子上面用记号笔写着名字。有时候她还会放一张纸条，写着「这个花椒是今年新摘的，特别香」或者「干辣椒可以先用油炸一下再用」。我把这些纸条都留着，夹在一本书里。调料抽屉里的东西在慢慢变化——用完的会被新的替换，偶尔也会加入一些以前没用过的。但无论怎么变，打开这个抽屉的那一刻，那种混合的香气总是让我想起家。那不是某一种具体的菜的味道，而是整个家的味道。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de chúfáng lǐ yǒu yí gè zhuānmén fàng tiáoliào de chōuti. Měi cì dǎkāi tā dōu néng wén dào gè zhǒng wèidào hùn zài yìqǐ de xiāngqì — bājiǎo de tián, huājiāo de má, gān làjiāo de là, hái yǒu guìpí nà zhǒng wēnnuǎn de mùtou wèi. Zhèxiē tiáoliào dà bùfen shì māma cóng lǎojiā jì lái de, tā juéde chāoshì mài de bù rú jiāxiāng de hǎo. Měi gé jǐ gè yuè tā jiù huì jì yí gè bāoguǒ, lǐmiàn zhuāng zhe yòng sùliào dài fēn zhuāng hǎo de gè zhǒng tiáoliào, měi gè dàizi shàngmiàn yòng jìhao bǐ xiě zhe míngzi. Yǒu shíhou tā hái huì fàng yì zhāng zhǐtiáo, xiě zhe「zhège huājiāo shì jīnnián xīn zhāi de, tèbié xiāng」huòzhě「gān làjiāo kěyǐ xiān yòng yóu zhá yíxià zài yòng」. Wǒ bǎ zhèxiē zhǐtiáo dōu liú zhe, jiā zài yì běn shū lǐ. Tiáoliào chōuti lǐ de dōngxi zài mànmàn biànhuà — yòng wán de huì bèi xīn de tìhuàn, ǒu'ěr yě huì jiā rù yìxiē yǐqián méi yòng guò de. Dàn wúlùn zěnme biàn, dǎkāi zhège chōuti de nà yí kè, nà zhǒng hùnhé de xiāngqì zǒngshì ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ jiā. Nà bú shì mǒu yì zhǒng jùtǐ de cài de wèidào, ér shì zhěng gè jiā de wèidào.
text_en: In my kitchen there's a drawer dedicated to spices. Every time I open it, a mixed fragrance hits me — the sweetness of star anise, the numbing of Sichuan peppercorn, the heat of dried chili, and the warm woody scent of cinnamon bark. Most of these spices were sent by Mom from our hometown — she thinks supermarket ones aren't as good. Every few months she sends a package, filled with various spices portioned into plastic bags, each labeled with a marker. Sometimes she tucks in a note: 'This Sichuan pepper was freshly picked this year, especially fragrant,' or 'Try frying the dried chilies in oil before using.' I keep all these notes, pressed between the pages of a book. The contents of the spice drawer slowly change — used-up ones get replaced, occasionally something new gets added. But no matter how it changes, the moment I open that drawer, that mixed fragrance always makes me think of home. It's not the smell of any particular dish — it's the smell of the entire home.

questions.q_zh: 调料大部分是从哪里来的？
questions.q_en: Where do most of the spices come from?

options.pinyin: māma cóng lǎojiā jì lái de

questions.q_zh: 作者把妈妈的纸条放在哪里了？
questions.q_en: Where does the author keep mom's notes?

options.pinyin: jiā zài yì běn shū lǐ

title: Washing Rice
text_zh: 做饭的第一步是淘米。把米放进电饭锅的内胆里，加水，用手轻轻搅动。水马上变成白色的，那是米上的淀粉。把水倒掉，再加水，再搅，再倒。一般淘两三次，水就变清了。这个过程很简单，简单到不需要思考，但我特别喜欢。冷水从手指间流过，米粒在水里轻轻碰撞，发出很小很小的声音。冬天的时候水很冰，手会被冻得有点红，但我还是不想用温水——总觉得用冷水淘出来的米饭更好吃，虽然这可能只是我的心理作用。淘好米以后加上水，水量刚好到手指第一个关节的位置。这是奶奶教我的方法，她说不管煮多少米，水到这个位置就刚好。我试过很多次，确实每次都恰到好处。按下电饭锅的按钮，等待大约半个小时。等米饭的时间刚好可以炒两个菜。这种节奏感，是做饭最让人安心的地方。
text_pinyin: Zuòfàn de dì yī bù shì táo mǐ. Bǎ mǐ fàng jìn diànfànguō de nèidǎn lǐ, jiā shuǐ, yòng shǒu qīngqīng jiǎodòng. Shuǐ mǎshàng biàn chéng báisè de, nà shì mǐ shàng de diànfěn. Bǎ shuǐ dào diào, zài jiā shuǐ, zài jiǎo, zài dào. Yìbān táo liǎng sān cì, shuǐ jiù biàn qīng le. Zhège guòchéng hěn jiǎndān, jiǎndān dào bù xūyào sīkǎo, dàn wǒ tèbié xǐhuan. Lěng shuǐ cóng shǒuzhǐ jiān liú guò, mǐ lì zài shuǐ lǐ qīngqīng pèngzhuàng, fāchū hěn xiǎo hěn xiǎo de shēngyīn. Dōngtiān de shíhou shuǐ hěn bīng, shǒu huì bèi dòng de yǒudiǎn hóng, dàn wǒ háishi bù xiǎng yòng wēn shuǐ — zǒng juéde yòng lěng shuǐ táo chūlái de mǐfàn gèng hǎochī, suīrán zhè kěnéng zhǐ shì wǒ de xīnlǐ zuòyòng. Táo hǎo mǐ yǐhòu jiā shàng shuǐ, shuǐ liàng gānghǎo dào shǒuzhǐ dì yī gè guānjié de wèizhì. Zhè shì nǎinai jiāo wǒ de fāngfǎ, tā shuō bùguǎn zhǔ duōshǎo mǐ, shuǐ dào zhège wèizhì jiù gānghǎo. Wǒ shì guò hěn duō cì, quèshí měi cì dōu qià dào hǎo chù. Àn xià diànfànguō de ànniǔ, děngdài dàyuē bàn gè xiǎoshí. Děng mǐfàn de shíjiān gānghǎo kěyǐ chǎo liǎng gè cài. Zhè zhǒng jiézòu gǎn, shì zuòfàn zuì ràng rén ānxīn de dìfāng.
text_en: The first step of cooking is washing rice. Put the rice in the rice cooker's inner pot, add water, gently stir with your hand. The water immediately turns white — that's starch from the rice. Pour it out, add water again, stir again, pour again. Usually after two or three rinses, the water runs clear. The process is simple, so simple it requires no thought, but I especially love it. Cold water flowing between fingers, rice grains gently bumping in the water, making the tiniest sound. In winter the water is icy, hands turn a bit red from the cold, but I still don't want to use warm water — I always feel cold-water-washed rice tastes better, though this might just be psychological. After washing, add water to just the first knuckle of your finger. This is the method grandma taught me — she said no matter how much rice you cook, water to this level is just right. I've tried many times; it really is perfect every time. Press the rice cooker button, wait about half an hour. The waiting time is just enough to stir-fry two dishes. This sense of rhythm is what makes cooking most reassuring.

questions.q_zh: 淘米的时候水为什么变白了？
questions.q_en: Why does the water turn white when washing rice?

options.text_en: Because of starch on the rice

options.pinyin: shuǐ běnshēn jiù shì bái de

questions.q_zh: 奶奶教的加水方法是什么？
questions.q_en: What is grandma's method for measuring water?

options.pinyin: shuǐ dào shǒuzhǐ dì yī gè guānjié

options.text_en: Look at markings on the pot

title: The Vendor Who Remembers
text_zh: 菜市场里有一个卖鱼的大叔，我每次去都会在他那里买鱼。去了几次以后他就记住了我，远远看到我就会招手说「来了啊，今天的鲈鱼特别新鲜」。有时候我没打算买鱼，只是路过他的摊位，他也会和我聊几句，问我最近忙不忙，有没有好好吃饭。他的摊位永远是市场里最干净的一个。他每天早上三点多就去进货，挑最好的鱼，回来以后把冰铺好，鱼一条一条整齐地摆在上面。他帮客人杀鱼的动作特别利落，三两下就弄好了，还会主动问你要不要帮你把鱼切成块或者片。有一次我告诉他我在学做糖醋鱼，他不但帮我选了一条最合适的鱼，还详细地告诉我他妻子做糖醋鱼的方法。那天的糖醋鱼做得特别成功，我专门去告诉他，他笑得很开心。在这样的菜市场里买菜，买的不只是食材，还有一种人与人之间简单的善意。
text_pinyin: Càishìchǎng lǐ yǒu yí gè mài yú de dàshū, wǒ měi cì qù dōu huì zài tā nàlǐ mǎi yú. Qù le jǐ cì yǐhòu tā jiù jì zhù le wǒ, yuǎn yuǎn kàn dào wǒ jiù huì zhāo shǒu shuō「lái le a, jīntiān de lúyú tèbié xīnxiān」. Yǒu shíhou wǒ méi dǎsuàn mǎi yú, zhǐ shì lù guò tā de tānwèi, tā yě huì hé wǒ liáo jǐ jù, wèn wǒ zuìjìn máng bù máng, yǒu méiyǒu hǎohǎo chīfàn. Tā de tānwèi yǒngyuǎn shì shìchǎng lǐ zuì gānjìng de yí gè. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang sān diǎn duō jiù qù jìn huò, tiāo zuì hǎo de yú, huílái yǐhòu bǎ bīng pū hǎo, yú yì tiáo yì tiáo zhěngqí de bǎi zài shàngmiàn. Tā bāng kèrén shā yú de dòngzuò tèbié lìluo, sān liǎng xià jiù nòng hǎo le, hái huì zhǔdòng wèn nǐ yào bú yào bāng nǐ bǎ yú qiē chéng kuài huòzhě piàn. Yǒu yí cì wǒ gàosu tā wǒ zài xué zuò tángcù yú, tā búdàn bāng wǒ xuǎn le yì tiáo zuì héshì de yú, hái xiángxì de gàosu wǒ tā qīzi zuò tángcù yú de fāngfǎ. Nà tiān de tángcù yú zuò de tèbié chénggōng, wǒ zhuānmén qù gàosu tā, tā xiào de hěn kāixīn. Zài zhèyàng de càishìchǎng lǐ mǎi cài, mǎi de bù zhǐ shì shícái, hái yǒu yì zhǒng rén yǔ rén zhījiān jiǎndān de shànyì.
text_en: There's a fish vendor at the market — every time I go, I buy fish from him. After a few visits he remembered me; seeing me from afar he'd wave and say, 'You're here! Today's sea bass is especially fresh.' Sometimes I wasn't planning to buy fish, just passing his stall, and he'd still chat with me, asking if I've been busy, if I've been eating well. His stall is always the cleanest in the market. Every morning past 3 AM he goes to stock up, picks the best fish, comes back and lays out the ice, fish arranged neatly one by one. His fish-cleaning movements are swift — done in a few quick strokes — and he'll proactively ask if you want it cut into chunks or slices. Once I told him I was learning to make sweet and sour fish. He not only picked the most suitable fish for me, but also explained in detail his wife's method for making it. That day's sweet and sour fish turned out great; I went specifically to tell him, and he smiled happily. Shopping at a market like this, you're buying not just ingredients, but a kind of simple goodwill between people.

questions.q_zh: 卖鱼的大叔每天几点去进货？
questions.q_en: What time does the fish vendor go to stock up each day?

questions.q_zh: 作者做糖醋鱼的方法是谁教的？
questions.q_en: Who taught the author how to make sweet and sour fish?

options.text: 卖鱼大叔教了他妻子的做法
options.pinyin: mài yú dàshū jiāo le tā qīzi de zuòfǎ
options.text_en: The fish vendor shared his wife's method

title: The Smell of Bread
text_zh: 我住的小区附近有一家面包店，每天下午三点多就开始飘出烤面包的香味。那种味道是温暖的、甜甜的，像一条看不见的绳子把你往店里拉。面包店不大，但每一样都是老板自己做的。他是一个四十岁左右的男人，以前在一家大公司工作，后来辞职去学了烘焙，回来开了这家店。他说他不想做太多种类，就做几种最基本的面包，但每一种都要做到最好。他的法式面包外面很脆，里面很软，有一种淡淡的麦香。吐司也好吃，切片以后烤一下，涂上黄油，就是最完美的早餐。我喜欢在下午三点多路过的时候进去坐一会儿。店里有一张小桌子和两把椅子，可以坐着吃一块刚出炉的面包，喝一杯咖啡。面包店里永远是温暖的，不管外面是什么天气。这种温暖不只是因为烤箱的热，还因为一个人在认真做一件事的那种专注和踏实。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de xiǎoqū fùjìn yǒu yì jiā miànbāo diàn, měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn duō jiù kāishǐ piāo chū kǎo miànbāo de xiāngwèi. Nà zhǒng wèidào shì wēnnuǎn de, tián tián de, xiàng yì tiáo kàn bú jiàn de shéngzi bǎ nǐ wǎng diàn lǐ lā. Miànbāo diàn bú dà, dàn měi yí yàng dōu shì lǎobǎn zìjǐ zuò de. Tā shì yí gè sìshí suì zuǒyòu de nánrén, yǐqián zài yì jiā dà gōngsī gōngzuò, hòulái cízhí qù xué le hōngbèi, huílái kāi le zhè jiā diàn. Tā shuō tā bù xiǎng zuò tài duō zhǒnglèi, jiù zuò jǐ zhǒng zuì jīběn de miànbāo, dàn měi yì zhǒng dōu yào zuò dào zuì hǎo. Tā de Fǎshì miànbāo wàimiàn hěn cuì, lǐmiàn hěn ruǎn, yǒu yì zhǒng dàn dàn de màixiāng. Tǔsī yě hǎochī, qiē piàn yǐhòu kǎo yíxià, tú shàng huángyóu, jiù shì zuì wánměi de zǎocān. Wǒ xǐhuan zài xiàwǔ sān diǎn duō lùguò de shíhou jìnqù zuò yíhuìr. Diàn lǐ yǒu yì zhāng xiǎo zhuōzi hé liǎng bǎ yǐzi, kěyǐ zuò zhe chī yí kuài gāng chū lú de miànbāo, hē yì bēi kāfēi. Miànbāo diàn lǐ yǒngyuǎn shì wēnnuǎn de, bùguǎn wàimiàn shì shénme tiānqì. Zhè zhǒng wēnnuǎn bù zhǐ shì yīnwèi kǎoxiāng de rè, hái yīnwèi yí gè rén zài rènzhēn zuò yí jiàn shì de nà zhǒng zhuānzhù hé tāshi.
text_en: Near my neighborhood there's a bakery that starts filling the air with the smell of baking bread past 3 PM every day. That scent is warm and sweet, like an invisible thread pulling you into the shop. The bakery isn't big, but everything is made by the owner himself. He's a man around forty who used to work at a large company, then quit to learn baking and came back to open this shop. He says he doesn't want to make too many kinds — just a few basic breads, but each one has to be the best it can be. His French bread is crispy on the outside, soft inside, with a faint wheat fragrance. The toast is good too — slice and toast it, spread with butter, and it's the perfect breakfast. I like to stop in when passing by around 3 PM. There's a small table and two chairs where you can sit and eat a piece of freshly baked bread with a cup of coffee. The bakery is always warm, no matter the weather outside. That warmth isn't just from the oven's heat — it comes from a person's focus and groundedness in doing one thing seriously.

questions.q_zh: 面包店老板以前做什么工作？
questions.q_en: What did the bakery owner do before?

questions.q_zh: 老板对面包的态度是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the owner's attitude toward bread?

options.text: 做几种最基本的但做到最好
options.pinyin: zuò jǐ zhǒng zuì jīběn de dàn zuò dào zuì hǎo
options.text_en: Make a few basics but make them the best

title: Cutting Vegetables Slowly
text_zh: 有些人觉得切菜是做饭里最无聊的部分，但我觉得这是最让人放松的一步。把所有食材洗干净，放在案板上，一样一样地切。切土豆的时候，先切成片，再切成丝，尽量让每一根丝都差不多粗细。切胡萝卜的时候，可以切成圆片也可以切成滚刀块，不同的切法适合不同的做法。切葱花的时候，葱的香味会慢慢散发出来，清清的、淡淡的。我喜欢用一把好刀，锋利的刀切起来很顺畅，每一刀下去都很干脆。切菜的过程让我的注意力完全集中在手上，脑子里不再想工作的事，不再想明天要做什么。只有刀和案板，只有食材在手里慢慢变成我想要的形状。这种专注本身就是一种休息。等所有的菜都切好了，整齐地摆在小盘子里，还没开始炒，心情就已经变好了。准备的过程和做出来的结果一样重要。
text_pinyin: Yǒuxiē rén juéde qiē cài shì zuòfàn lǐ zuì wúliáo de bùfen, dàn wǒ juéde zhè shì zuì ràng rén fàngsōng de yí bù. Bǎ suǒyǒu shícái xǐ gānjìng, fàng zài ànbǎn shàng, yí yàng yí yàng de qiē. Qiē tǔdòu de shíhou, xiān qiē chéng piàn, zài qiē chéng sī, jǐnliàng ràng měi yì gēn sī dōu chàbuduō cū xì. Qiē húluóbo de shíhou, kěyǐ qiē chéng yuán piàn yě kěyǐ qiē chéng gǔndāo kuài, bùtóng de qiēfǎ shìhé bùtóng de zuòfǎ. Qiē cōnghuā de shíhou, cōng de xiāngwèi huì mànmàn sànfā chūlái, qīng qīng de, dàn dàn de. Wǒ xǐhuan yòng yì bǎ hǎo dāo, fēnglì de dāo qiē qǐlái hěn shùnchàng, měi yí dāo xià qù dōu hěn gāncuì. Qiē cài de guòchéng ràng wǒ de zhùyìlì wánquán jízhōng zài shǒu shàng, nǎozi lǐ bú zài xiǎng gōngzuò de shì, bú zài xiǎng míngtiān yào zuò shénme. Zhǐ yǒu dāo hé ànbǎn, zhǐ yǒu shícái zài shǒu lǐ mànmàn biàn chéng wǒ xiǎng yào de xíngzhuàng. Zhè zhǒng zhuānzhù běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng xiūxi. Děng suǒyǒu de cài dōu qiē hǎo le, zhěngqí de bǎi zài xiǎo pánzi lǐ, hái méi kāishǐ chǎo, xīnqíng jiù yǐjīng biàn hǎo le. Zhǔnbèi de guòchéng hé zuò chūlái de jiéguǒ yíyàng zhòngyào.
text_en: Some people think chopping vegetables is the most boring part of cooking, but I think it's the most relaxing step. Wash all the ingredients, place them on the cutting board, and cut them one by one. When cutting potatoes, first slice, then julienne, trying to keep each strand roughly the same thickness. When cutting carrots, you can do round slices or rolling-knife chunks — different cuts suit different cooking methods. When chopping scallions, their fragrance slowly releases, light and delicate. I like using a good knife — a sharp knife cuts smoothly, each stroke clean and decisive. The process of cutting focuses my attention entirely on my hands. My mind stops thinking about work, stops thinking about tomorrow. Just the knife and the board, just ingredients slowly taking the shape I want. That focus itself is a form of rest. Once everything is cut, arranged neatly in small dishes, before I've even started cooking, my mood has already improved. The process of preparation is as important as the finished result.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得切菜有什么好处？
questions.q_en: What benefit does the author find in chopping vegetables?

options.text: 注意力集中，不再想工作
options.pinyin: zhùyìlì jízhōng, bú zài xiǎng gōngzuò
cài qiē de hǎokàn gèng hǎochī

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得做饭中什么和结果一样重要？
questions.q_en: What does the author think is as important as the result?

title: A Bowl of Noodles in the Rain
text_zh: 那天下了很大的雨，我忘了带伞，从地铁站跑到家的时候已经全身湿透了。换了干衣服以后，还是觉得很冷，身体里面好像有一种寒气赶不走。我决定给自己煮一碗热面条。锅里烧上水，找出家里的挂面。等水开了，把面条放进去，煮两三分钟。同时在另一个小锅里热了一些昨天的鸡汤，打了一个鸡蛋进去，等蛋白凝固了捞出来。面条煮好以后捞到碗里，浇上热鸡汤，放上荷包蛋，撒一点葱花和白芝麻。端着碗坐到沙发上，喝了第一口汤的时候，那种暖意从喉咙一直到了胃里，然后慢慢扩散到全身。窗外的雨还在下，但我已经不觉得冷了。面条很普通，但在那个时刻，它就是全世界最好吃的东西。有时候食物的意义不在于它本身有多特别，而在于你在什么时候遇到了它。
text_pinyin: Nà tiān xià le hěn dà de yǔ, wǒ wàng le dài sǎn, cóng dìtiě zhàn pǎo dào jiā de shíhou yǐjīng quánshēn shī tòu le. Huàn le gān yīfu yǐhòu, háishi juéde hěn lěng, shēntǐ lǐmiàn hǎoxiàng yǒu yì zhǒng hánqì gǎn bù zǒu. Wǒ juédìng gěi zìjǐ zhǔ yì wǎn rè miàntiáo. Guō lǐ shāo shàng shuǐ, zhǎo chū jiā lǐ de guàmiàn. Děng shuǐ kāi le, bǎ miàntiáo fàng jìnqù, zhǔ liǎng sān fēnzhōng. Tóngshí zài lìng yí gè xiǎo guō lǐ rè le yìxiē zuótiān de jītāng, dǎ le yí gè jīdàn jìnqù, děng dànbái nínggù le lāo chūlái. Miàntiáo zhǔ hǎo yǐhòu lāo dào wǎn lǐ, jiāo shàng rè jītāng, fàng shàng hébāo dàn, sā yìdiǎn cōnghuā hé bái zhīma. Duān zhe wǎn zuò dào shāfā shàng, hē le dì yī kǒu tāng de shíhou, nà zhǒng nuǎn yì cóng hóulóng yìzhí dào le wèi lǐ, ránhòu mànmàn kuòsàn dào quánshēn. Chuāng wài de yǔ hái zài xià, dàn wǒ yǐjīng bù juéde lěng le. Miàntiáo hěn pǔtōng, dàn zài nàge shíkè, tā jiù shì quán shìjiè zuì hǎochī de dōngxi. Yǒu shíhou shíwù de yìyì bú zài yú tā běnshēn yǒu duō tèbié, ér zài yú nǐ zài shénme shíhou yù dào le tā.
text_en: It rained heavily that day. I forgot my umbrella, and by the time I ran from the subway to home I was completely soaked. After changing into dry clothes, I still felt cold — as if a chill inside my body wouldn't leave. I decided to cook myself a bowl of hot noodles. Put water on to boil, found some dried noodles. When the water boiled, dropped them in, cooked two or three minutes. Meanwhile in another small pot I heated some of yesterday's chicken broth, cracked an egg in, waited for the white to set and scooped it out. Drained the noodles into a bowl, ladled on hot chicken broth, placed the poached egg on top, sprinkled some scallion and white sesame. Holding the bowl on the sofa, the first sip of broth — that warmth traveled from throat to stomach, then slowly spread through my whole body. Rain still falling outside, but I no longer felt cold. The noodles were ordinary, but in that moment they were the most delicious thing in the world. Sometimes food's meaning isn't in how special it is, but in when you encounter it.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么全身湿透了？
questions.q_en: Why was the author soaked?

options.text: 忘了带伞在雨里跑回家
options.pinyin: wàng le dài sǎn zài yǔ lǐ pǎo huí jiā
options.text_en: Forgot umbrella and ran home in rain

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得食物的意义在于什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think food's meaning lies in?

options.text: 你在什么时候遇到了它
options.pinyin: nǐ zài shénme shíhou yù dào le tā

title: Fermenting Time
text_zh: 上个月我开始学做面包，才发现做面包最重要的一步不是揉面，而是等待。把面粉、水、酵母和盐混合在一起，揉成一个光滑的面团，然后盖上湿布，放在温暖的地方，等它慢慢发酵。这个过程需要一到两个小时，有时候更长。你什么都不需要做，只是等。等待的时候我会看看书或者做做家务，偶尔去看一眼面团有没有变大。每次揭开布看到面团涨到原来两倍大的时候，都会有一种小小的惊喜。酵母是一种活的生物，它在面团里慢慢地工作，产生气泡，让面团变得蓬松。这个过程不能着急，温度太高面团会发酸，温度太低又发不起来。你只能给它合适的条件，然后相信时间会做好它的工作。做面包教会了我一件事：有些东西是催不出来的，你只能耐心等待，让它按照自己的节奏完成。
text_pinyin: Shàng gè yuè wǒ kāishǐ xué zuò miànbāo, cái fāxiàn zuò miànbāo zuì zhòngyào de yí bù bú shì róu miàn, ér shì děngdài. Bǎ miànfěn, shuǐ, jiàomǔ hé yán hùnhé zài yìqǐ, róu chéng yí gè guānghuá de miàntuán, ránhòu gài shàng shī bù, fàng zài wēnnuǎn de dìfāng, děng tā mànmàn fājiào. Zhège guòchéng xūyào yí dào liǎng gè xiǎoshí, yǒu shíhou gèng cháng. Nǐ shénme dōu bù xūyào zuò, zhǐ shì děng. Děngdài de shíhou wǒ huì kànkan shū huòzhě zuòzuo jiāwù, ǒu'ěr qù kàn yì yǎn miàntuán yǒu méiyǒu biàn dà. Měi cì jiē kāi bù kàn dào miàntuán zhǎng dào yuánlái liǎng bèi dà de shíhou, dōu huì yǒu yì zhǒng xiǎo xiǎo de jīngxǐ. Jiàomǔ shì yì zhǒng huó de shēngwù, tā zài miàntuán lǐ mànmàn de gōngzuò, chǎnshēng qìpào, ràng miàntuán biàn de péng sōng. Zhège guòchéng bù néng zhāojí, wēndù tài gāo miàntuán huì fā suān, wēndù tài dī yòu fā bù qǐlái. Nǐ zhǐ néng gěi tā héshì de tiáojiàn, ránhòu xiāngxìn shíjiān huì zuò hǎo tā de gōngzuò. Zuò miànbāo jiāo huì le wǒ yí jiàn shì: yǒuxiē dōngxi shì cuī bù chūlái de, nǐ zhǐ néng nàixīn děngdài, ràng tā ànzhào zìjǐ de jiézòu wánchéng.
text_en: Last month I started learning to bake bread and discovered the most important step isn't kneading — it's waiting. Mix flour, water, yeast, and salt together, knead into a smooth dough, then cover with a damp cloth, place somewhere warm, and let it slowly ferment. This takes one to two hours, sometimes longer. You don't need to do anything — just wait. While waiting I'd read or do housework, occasionally checking if the dough has grown. Every time I lift the cloth and see it's doubled in size, there's a small thrill. Yeast is a living organism, working slowly inside the dough, producing bubbles, making it fluffy. The process can't be rushed — too warm and the dough turns sour, too cool and it won't rise. You can only provide the right conditions, then trust time to do its work. Bread-making taught me something: some things can't be hurried. You can only wait patiently, letting them complete at their own pace.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得做面包最重要的一步是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think is the most important step in making bread?

questions.q_zh: 温度太高面团会怎么样？
questions.q_en: What happens if the temperature is too high?

title: Lunch with an Old Colleague
text_zh: 昨天和一个三年没见的老同事约了午饭。她现在换了一份新工作，比以前轻松一些，人看起来也比以前开朗了。我们去了一家安静的日式餐厅，点了两份定食。等菜的时候聊了聊各自的近况，说着说着就说到了以前一起加班的日子。那时候我们经常一起加班到晚上十点，然后去公司楼下的便利店买关东煮。两个人站在便利店门口吃着热乎乎的萝卜和豆腐，说着明天还要干什么。那些日子虽然累，但回忆起来竟然有一种温暖的感觉。菜上来了，我们边吃边聊。她说她最近开始学烹饪了，周末会花半天的时间做一顿饭，慢慢吃。「以前觉得做饭浪费时间，」她说，「现在觉得那是给自己最好的时间。」我们吃了两个小时才离开。分别的时候她说下次她做饭请我吃。走在回去的路上，我觉得很幸运，有一些朋友即使很久不见，一坐下来就能接上以前的话题。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān hé yí gè sān nián méi jiàn de lǎo tóngshì yuē le wǔfàn. Tā xiànzài huàn le yí fèn xīn gōngzuò, bǐ yǐqián qīngsōng yìxiē, rén kàn qǐlái yě bǐ yǐqián kāilǎng le. Wǒmen qù le yì jiā ānjìng de Rìshì cāntīng, diǎn le liǎng fèn dìngshí. Děng cài de shíhou liáo le liáo gèzì de jìnkuàng, shuō zhe shuō zhe jiù shuō dào le yǐqián yìqǐ jiābān de rìzi. Nà shíhou wǒmen jīngcháng yìqǐ jiābān dào wǎnshang shí diǎn, ránhòu qù gōngsī lóu xià de biànlì diàn mǎi guāndōng zhǔ. Liǎng gè rén zhàn zài biànlì diàn ménkǒu chī zhe rè hūhū de luóbo hé dòufu, shuō zhe míngtiān hái yào gàn shénme. Nàxiē rìzi suīrán lèi, dàn huíyì qǐlái jìngrán yǒu yì zhǒng wēnnuǎn de gǎnjué. Cài shàng lái le, wǒmen biān chī biān liáo. Tā shuō tā zuìjìn kāishǐ xué pēngrèn le, zhōumò huì huā bàn tiān de shíjiān zuò yí dùn fàn, mànmàn chī.「Yǐqián juéde zuòfàn làngfèi shíjiān,」tā shuō,「xiànzài juéde nà shì gěi zìjǐ zuì hǎo de shíjiān.」Wǒmen chī le liǎng gè xiǎoshí cái líkāi. Fēnbié de shíhou tā shuō xià cì tā zuòfàn qǐng wǒ chī. Zǒu zài huíqù de lù shàng, wǒ juéde hěn xìngyùn, yǒu yìxiē péngyou jíshǐ hěn jiǔ bú jiàn, yí zuò xiàlái jiù néng jiē shàng yǐqián de huàtí.
text_en: Yesterday I had lunch with a former colleague I hadn't seen in three years. She has a new job now, more relaxed than before, and she looks brighter. We went to a quiet Japanese restaurant and ordered two set meals. While waiting we caught up, and the conversation drifted to our old days of working overtime together. Back then we'd often work until 10 PM, then go to the convenience store downstairs for oden. Standing outside the store eating hot radish and tofu, talking about what we had to do tomorrow. Those days were tiring, but looking back, there's a surprising warmth to them. The food arrived and we ate and talked. She said she's recently started learning to cook — spending half a day on weekends making a meal, eating slowly. 'I used to think cooking was a waste of time,' she said. 'Now I think it's the best time I give myself.' We didn't leave for two hours. When we parted she said next time she'd cook for me. Walking back, I felt lucky — some friends, even after years apart, can pick right up where the conversation left off the moment you sit down.

questions.q_zh: 以前加班以后他们去哪里吃东西？
questions.q_en: Where did they eat after working overtime before?

options.pinyin: gōngsī lóu xià de biànlì diàn

questions.q_zh: 老同事现在觉得做饭是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the old colleague now think cooking is?

options.pinyin: gěi zìjǐ zuì hǎo de shíjiān

title: The Egg That Took Practice
text_zh: 煎一个完美的荷包蛋比想象的要难。我刚开始学做饭的时候，以为煎蛋是最简单的事情——锅里放油，打个蛋，等一等就好了。结果不是焦了就是散了，要么蛋黄已经全熟了但蛋白下面还是生的。后来我上网查了一下，才知道煎蛋有很多讲究。首先油的温度很重要，太热蛋一下去就会起泡，边缘变焦；太冷又粘锅。最好的方法是中小火热锅，加一点油，等油微微冒烟的时候把蛋打进去。然后不要急着翻面，让蛋白慢慢凝固。如果想要蛋黄还是流动的，大概煎两分钟就够了；如果想要全熟，可以盖上锅盖焖一分钟。我练了大概一个星期，每天早上煎一个蛋，终于煎出了让自己满意的荷包蛋——蛋白底部微微焦黄，蛋黄还是流动的，撒一点盐和黑胡椒就很好吃。一个小小的荷包蛋，让我明白了一件事：即使是最简单的东西，要做好也需要反复练习。
text_pinyin: Jiān yí gè wánměi de hébāo dàn bǐ xiǎngxiàng de yào nán. Wǒ gāng kāishǐ xué zuòfàn de shíhou, yǐwéi jiān dàn shì zuì jiǎndān de shìqing — guō lǐ fàng yóu, dǎ gè dàn, děng yì děng jiù hǎo le. Jiéguǒ bú shì jiāo le jiù shì sàn le, yàome dànhuáng yǐjīng quán shú le dàn dànbái xiàmiàn háishi shēng de. Hòulái wǒ shàng wǎng chá le yíxià, cái zhīdào jiān dàn yǒu hěn duō jiǎngjiū. Shǒuxiān yóu de wēndù hěn zhòngyào, tài rè dàn yí xià qù jiù huì qǐ pào, biānyuán biàn jiāo; tài lěng yòu zhān guō. Zuì hǎo de fāngfǎ shì zhōng xiǎo huǒ rè guō, jiā yìdiǎn yóu, děng yóu wēiwēi mào yān de shíhou bǎ dàn dǎ jìnqù. Ránhòu bú yào jí zhe fān miàn, ràng dànbái mànmàn nínggù. Rúguǒ xiǎng yào dànhuáng háishi liúdòng de, dàgài jiān liǎng fēnzhōng jiù gòu le; rúguǒ xiǎng yào quán shú, kěyǐ gài shàng guō gài mèn yì fēnzhōng. Wǒ liàn le dàgài yí gè xīngqī, měi tiān zǎoshang jiān yí gè dàn, zhōngyú jiān chū le ràng zìjǐ mǎnyì de hébāo dàn — dànbái dǐbù wēiwēi jiāo huáng, dànhuáng háishi liúdòng de, sā yìdiǎn yán hé hēi hújiāo jiù hěn hǎochī. Yí gè xiǎo xiǎo de hébāo dàn, ràng wǒ míngbai le yí jiàn shì: jíshǐ shì zuì jiǎndān de dōngxi, yào zuò hǎo yě xūyào fǎnfù liànxí.
text_en: Frying a perfect sunny-side-up egg is harder than you'd think. When I first started cooking, I thought frying an egg was the simplest thing — oil in the pan, crack an egg, wait and done. But it either burned or fell apart, or the yolk was fully cooked while the white underneath was still raw. Later I looked it up online and discovered there's a lot to it. First, oil temperature matters — too hot and the egg bubbles immediately, edges burn; too cold and it sticks. The best method is medium-low heat, a bit of oil, and when the oil just barely smokes, crack the egg in. Then don't rush to flip — let the white slowly set. For a runny yolk, about two minutes is enough; for fully cooked, cover with a lid for one minute. I practiced about a week, frying one egg every morning, and finally made an egg I was satisfied with — white slightly golden on the bottom, yolk still runny, sprinkle of salt and black pepper and it's delicious. One small fried egg taught me something: even the simplest things require repeated practice to do well.

questions.q_zh: 刚开始煎蛋的时候出了什么问题？
questions.q_en: What went wrong when first frying eggs?

options.pinyin: bú shì jiāo le jiù shì sàn le

questions.q_zh: 作者练了多长时间才学会煎蛋？
questions.q_en: How long did the author practice before learning to fry eggs?

title: Tea Eggs on the Train
text_zh: 小时候坐火车回老家，车程大概八个小时。那时候还没有高铁，绿皮火车慢慢地穿过一个又一个小站。车厢里总是有一种特别的味道——方便面、橘子皮，还有茶叶蛋。卖茶叶蛋的阿姨推着一个小车，从车厢的一头走到另一头，一边走一边喊「茶叶蛋、茶叶蛋，热乎的茶叶蛋」。那声音在嘈杂的车厢里特别好听。茶叶蛋是用茶叶、八角、桂皮和酱油一起煮的，蛋壳上有一道一道的裂纹，像是一幅画。剥开以后蛋白是深棕色的，有一种特别的咸香味。咬一口，蛋黄粉粉的，配上那种茶叶和香料的味道，比家里煮的白水蛋好吃太多了。我每次坐火车都会买两个。到了深夜，大部分人都睡了，车厢的灯暗了下来，只有车轮碰到铁轨的声音。偶尔醒来，看看窗外黑黑的夜，再闻闻手上还残留的茶叶蛋的味道，就觉得很安心。那种安心和现在坐高铁的感觉完全不一样。
text_pinyin: Xiǎo shíhou zuò huǒchē huí lǎojiā, chēchéng dàgài bā gè xiǎoshí. Nà shíhou hái méiyǒu gāotiě, lǜpí huǒchē mànmàn de chuān guò yí gè yòu yí gè xiǎo zhàn. Chēxiāng lǐ zǒngshì yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié de wèidào — fāngbiàn miàn, júzi pí, hái yǒu cháyè dàn. Mài cháyè dàn de āyí tuī zhe yí gè xiǎo chē, cóng chēxiāng de yì tóu zǒu dào lìng yì tóu, yìbiān zǒu yìbiān hǎn「cháyè dàn, cháyè dàn, rè hū de cháyè dàn」. Nà shēngyīn zài cáozá de chēxiāng lǐ tèbié hǎotīng. Cháyè dàn shì yòng cháyè, bājiǎo, guìpí hé jiàngyóu yìqǐ zhǔ de, dànké shàng yǒu yí dào yí dào de lièwén, xiàng shì yì fú huà. Bāo kāi yǐhòu dànbái shì shēn zōngsè de, yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié de xián xiāng wèi. Yǎo yì kǒu, dànhuáng fěn fěn de, pèi shàng nà zhǒng cháyè hé xiāngliào de wèidào, bǐ jiā lǐ zhǔ de bái shuǐ dàn hǎochī tài duō le. Wǒ měi cì zuò huǒchē dōu huì mǎi liǎng gè. Dào le shēnyè, dà bùfen rén dōu shuì le, chēxiāng de dēng àn le xiàlái, zhǐ yǒu chēlún pèng dào tiěguǐ de shēngyīn. Ǒu'ěr xǐng lái, kànkan chuāng wài hēi hēi de yè, zài wén wen shǒu shàng hái cánliú de cháyè dàn de wèidào, jiù juéde hěn ānxīn. Nà zhǒng ānxīn hé xiànzài zuò gāotiě de gǎnjué wánquán bù yíyàng.
text_en: As a child, taking the train back to my hometown was about eight hours. High-speed rail didn't exist yet; the old green trains crawled through station after station. The carriages always had a distinctive smell — instant noodles, orange peels, and tea eggs. The auntie selling tea eggs pushed a small cart from one end of the car to the other, calling out, 'Tea eggs, tea eggs, hot tea eggs.' Her voice sounded wonderful amid the carriage noise. Tea eggs are simmered with tea leaves, star anise, cinnamon bark, and soy sauce. The shells have a web of cracks, like a painting. Peeled, the white is deep brown with a special savory fragrance. One bite — the yolk is powdery, paired with the tea and spice flavors, so much better than plain boiled eggs at home. I'd buy two every train ride. Late at night, most people asleep, carriage lights dimmed, only the sound of wheels on tracks. Waking occasionally, looking at the dark night outside, smelling the lingering tea egg scent on my hands, I felt at peace. That kind of peace is nothing like riding high-speed rail today.

questions.q_zh: 回老家坐火车要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long was the train ride home?

questions.q_zh: 茶叶蛋是用什么煮的？
questions.q_en: What are tea eggs cooked with?

options.text_en: Tea, star anise, cinnamon, and soy sauce

title: The Tofu Seller's Philosophy
text_zh: 早市里卖豆腐的老张每天凌晨三点就开始做豆腐了。他做了三十多年，从他父亲那里学来的手艺。我有一次很早去市场，看到他的摊位还在准备。他正把刚做好的豆腐从模具里小心地拿出来，一块一块地摆好。我问他做了这么多年不觉得无聊吗。他想了一下说，「每天做的豆腐都不完全一样。水的温度、黄豆的品质、天气的变化，都会影响最后的结果。做了三十年我还在学习怎么做得更好。」他又说，「有些人觉得豆腐就是豆腐，没什么特别的。但如果你仔细尝，好的豆腐有一种淡淡的甜味，口感滑嫩，入口即化。做到这个程度不容易。」我买了一块他的豆腐回家，没有做任何复杂的菜，就是凉拌豆腐——切成块，放上葱花、香菜、酱油和香油。一口吃下去，确实有一种淡淡的甜。我想起老张的话，觉得他说的不只是豆腐，也是一种对生活的态度。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshì lǐ mài dòufu de Lǎo Zhāng měi tiān língchén sān diǎn jiù kāishǐ zuò dòufu le. Tā zuò le sānshí duō nián, cóng tā fùqīn nàlǐ xué lái de shǒuyì. Wǒ yǒu yí cì hěn zǎo qù shìchǎng, kàn dào tā de tānwèi hái zài zhǔnbèi. Tā zhèng bǎ gāng zuò hǎo de dòufu cóng mújù lǐ xiǎoxīn de ná chūlái, yí kuài yí kuài de bǎi hǎo. Wǒ wèn tā zuò le zhème duō nián bù juéde wúliáo ma. Tā xiǎng le yíxià shuō,「měi tiān zuò de dòufu dōu bù wánquán yíyàng. Shuǐ de wēndù, huángdòu de pǐnzhì, tiānqì de biànhuà, dōu huì yǐngxiǎng zuìhòu de jiéguǒ. Zuò le sānshí nián wǒ hái zài xuéxí zěnme zuò de gèng hǎo.」Tā yòu shuō,「yǒuxiē rén juéde dòufu jiù shì dòufu, méi shénme tèbié de. Dàn rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì cháng, hǎo de dòufu yǒu yì zhǒng dàn dàn de tián wèi, kǒugǎn huá nèn, rù kǒu jí huà. Zuò dào zhège chéngdù bù róngyì.」Wǒ mǎi le yí kuài tā de dòufu huí jiā, méiyǒu zuò rènhé fùzá de cài, jiù shì liángbàn dòufu — qiē chéng kuài, fàng shàng cōnghuā, xiāngcài, jiàngyóu hé xiāngyóu. Yì kǒu chī xiàqù, quèshí yǒu yì zhǒng dàn dàn de tián. Wǒ xiǎng qǐ Lǎo Zhāng de huà, juéde tā shuō de bù zhǐ shì dòufu, yě shì yì zhǒng duì shēnghuó de tàidù.
text_en: Old Zhang, the tofu seller at the morning market, starts making tofu at 3 AM every day. He's been at it over thirty years, a craft learned from his father. Once I went to the market very early and saw his stall still being set up. He was carefully removing freshly made tofu from the mold, arranging blocks one by one. I asked if thirty years of this wasn't boring. He thought for a moment: 'The tofu I make every day isn't exactly the same. Water temperature, soybean quality, weather changes — they all affect the final result. Thirty years and I'm still learning to do it better.' He added, 'Some people think tofu is just tofu, nothing special. But if you taste carefully, good tofu has a faint sweetness, a silky texture, melts in your mouth. Reaching that level isn't easy.' I bought a block of his tofu and went home. No complicated dish — just cold-dressed tofu, cut into pieces with scallion, cilantro, soy sauce, and sesame oil. One bite, and indeed there was a faint sweetness. I thought of Old Zhang's words and felt he wasn't just talking about tofu — it was an attitude toward life.

questions.q_zh: 老张的手艺是从谁那里学来的？
questions.q_en: Who did Old Zhang learn his craft from?

questions.q_zh: 老张觉得做了三十年还在学什么？
questions.q_en: What does Old Zhang feel he's still learning after thirty years?

title: The Smell of Home
text_zh: 过年回家的第一件事不是放下行李，不是和爸妈打招呼，而是深深地吸一口气。家的味道是一种混合的气息——木地板的味道、阳台上晒过的被子的味道、厨房里飘出来的饭菜香。这种味道在外面是闻不到的，只有推开家门的那一刻才能完整地感受到。每次闻到的时候我都会愣一下，然后心里有一种说不出的放松。妈妈总是在我到家之前就开始做饭了。她会做我最爱吃的几道菜：红烧鱼、炒腊肉、凉拌豆皮，还有一个蛋花汤。这些菜在外面的餐厅都能吃到，但味道就是不一样。也许差别不在于食材或者做法，而在于做菜的那个人。知道你要回来，提前想好你爱吃什么，一大早去市场买最新鲜的食材，花一整个下午慢慢准备。这些事情不会写在菜谱里，但它们才是「家的味道」真正的配方。坐在桌前吃第一口饭的时候，一切旅途的疲惫都消失了。
text_pinyin: Guònián huí jiā de dì yī jiàn shì bú shì fàng xià xíngli, bú shì hé bà mā dǎ zhāohu, ér shì shēn shēn de xī yì kǒu qì. Jiā de wèidào shì yì zhǒng hùnhé de qìxī — mù dìbǎn de wèidào, yángtái shàng shài guò de bèizi de wèidào, chúfáng lǐ piāo chūlái de fàncài xiāng. Zhè zhǒng wèidào zài wàimiàn shì wén bú dào de, zhǐ yǒu tuī kāi jiā mén de nà yí kè cái néng wánzhěng de gǎnshòu dào. Měi cì wén dào de shíhou wǒ dōu huì lèng yíxià, ránhòu xīnlǐ yǒu yì zhǒng shuō bu chū de fàngsōng. Māma zǒngshì zài wǒ dào jiā zhīqián jiù kāishǐ zuòfàn le. Tā huì zuò wǒ zuì ài chī de jǐ dào cài: hóngshāo yú, chǎo làròu, liángbàn dòupí, hái yǒu yí gè dànhuā tāng. Zhèxiē cài zài wàimiàn de cāntīng dōu néng chī dào, dàn wèidào jiù shì bù yíyàng. Yěxǔ chābié bú zài yú shícái huòzhě zuòfǎ, ér zài yú zuò cài de nàge rén. Zhīdào nǐ yào huílái, tíqián xiǎng hǎo nǐ ài chī shénme, yí dà zǎo qù shìchǎng mǎi zuì xīnxiān de shícái, huā yí zhěng gè xiàwǔ mànmàn zhǔnbèi. Zhèxiē shìqing bú huì xiě zài càipǔ lǐ, dàn tāmen cái shì「jiā de wèidào」zhēnzhèng de pèifāng. Zuò zài zhuō qián chī dì yī kǒu fàn de shíhou, yíqiè lǚtú de píbèi dōu xiāoshī le.
text_en: The first thing about coming home for New Year isn't putting down luggage or greeting your parents — it's taking a deep breath. The smell of home is a blended scent — wood floors, sun-dried quilts from the balcony, cooking fragrance drifting from the kitchen. You can't smell this anywhere else; only the moment you push open the front door can you fully feel it. Every time I catch it I pause, then feel an indescribable relaxation. Mom always starts cooking before I arrive. She makes my favorite dishes: braised fish, stir-fried cured meat, cold-dressed tofu skin, and egg drop soup. You can eat all these at restaurants outside, but the taste is just different. Perhaps the difference isn't in the ingredients or the method, but in the person cooking. Knowing you're coming home, thinking ahead about what you like, going to the market early for the freshest ingredients, spending an entire afternoon preparing. These things won't be written in any recipe, but they are the true formula for 'the taste of home.' Sitting at the table, eating that first bite, all the fatigue of the journey vanishes.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得回家第一件事是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think is the first thing about coming home?

options.pinyin: shēn shēn de xī yì kǒu qì

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得「家的味道」真正的配方是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think is the real recipe for 'the taste of home'?

options.text: 做菜的人提前想好你爱吃什么慢慢准备
options.pinyin: zuò cài de rén tíqián xiǎng hǎo nǐ ài chī shénme mànmàn zhǔnbèi
options.text_en: The cook thinking ahead about what you like and preparing carefully

title: Leftovers for Breakfast
text_zh: 我有一个可能不太健康但让我很开心的习惯：用昨晚的剩菜做早餐。把冰箱里剩下的菜拿出来，不管是什么——可能是半盘回锅肉、一小碗番茄炒蛋、几根炒青菜——全都倒进锅里和米饭一起炒。大火翻炒几分钟，让所有的味道混在一起，米饭被菜汁染成了各种颜色。盛到碗里，冒着热气，是这个世界上最随意但也最让人满足的早餐。我知道有些人觉得吃剩菜不好，但我觉得剩菜炒饭有一种特别的味道。那些菜在冰箱里待了一晚上，味道反而变得更浓了。不同的菜混在一起，有时候会产生意想不到的搭配。而且剩菜炒饭有一种节约的美感——什么都不浪费，所有的东西都被好好地利用了。吃着剩菜炒饭的时候，我常常想起小时候妈妈也是这样做的。她每天早上都会把昨晚的剩菜变成一锅好吃的炒饭。也许这不是什么高级的烹饪，但它是真实的、日常的、温暖的。这就够了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yí gè kěnéng bú tài jiànkāng dàn ràng wǒ hěn kāixīn de xíguàn: yòng zuó wǎn de shèng cài zuò zǎocān. Bǎ bīngxiāng lǐ shèng xià de cài ná chūlái, bùguǎn shì shénme — kěnéng shì bàn pán huíguō ròu, yì xiǎo wǎn fānqié chǎo dàn, jǐ gēn chǎo qīngcài — quán dōu dào jìn guō lǐ hé mǐfàn yìqǐ chǎo. Dà huǒ fānchǎo jǐ fēnzhōng, ràng suǒyǒu de wèidào hùn zài yìqǐ, mǐfàn bèi cài zhī rǎn chéng le gè zhǒng yánsè. Chéng dào wǎn lǐ, mào zhe rèqì, shì zhège shìjiè shàng zuì suíyì dàn yě zuì ràng rén mǎnzú de zǎocān. Wǒ zhīdào yǒuxiē rén juéde chī shèng cài bù hǎo, dàn wǒ juéde shèng cài chǎofàn yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié de wèidào. Nàxiē cài zài bīngxiāng lǐ dāi le yì wǎnshang, wèidào fǎn'ér biàn de gèng nóng le. Bùtóng de cài hùn zài yìqǐ, yǒu shíhou huì chǎnshēng yì xiǎng bú dào de dāpèi. Érqiě shèng cài chǎofàn yǒu yì zhǒng jiéyuē de měigǎn — shénme dōu bù làngfèi, suǒyǒu de dōngxi dōu bèi hǎohǎo de lìyòng le. Chī zhe shèng cài chǎofàn de shíhou, wǒ chángcháng xiǎng qǐ xiǎo shíhou māma yě shì zhèyàng zuò de. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang dōu huì bǎ zuó wǎn de shèng cài biàn chéng yì guō hǎochī de chǎofàn. Yěxǔ zhè bú shì shénme gāojí de pēngrèn, dàn tā shì zhēnshí de, rìcháng de, wēnnuǎn de. Zhè jiù gòu le.
text_en: I have a habit that might not be the healthiest but makes me very happy: using last night's leftovers for breakfast. Take whatever's left in the fridge — maybe half a plate of twice-cooked pork, a small bowl of scrambled eggs with tomato, a few stalks of stir-fried greens — dump it all in the wok with rice and stir-fry. High heat for a few minutes, letting all the flavors meld, the rice stained various colors by the sauce. Scooped into a bowl, steaming, it's the most casual but most satisfying breakfast in the world. I know some people think eating leftovers isn't great, but I think leftover fried rice has a special taste. Those dishes sitting overnight in the fridge actually develop deeper flavor. Different dishes mixed together sometimes create unexpected combinations. And there's an aesthetic of thrift to leftover fried rice — nothing wasted, everything put to good use. Eating it, I often think of how my mom did exactly this. Every morning she'd turn last night's leftovers into a delicious wok of fried rice. Maybe it's not haute cuisine, but it's real, it's everyday, it's warm. That's enough.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得剩菜在冰箱里放了一晚上以后怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What does the author think happens to leftovers after a night in the fridge?

options.pinyin: wèidào biàn de gèng nóng le

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得剩菜炒饭有什么美感？
questions.q_en: What aesthetic does the author find in leftover fried rice?
